ingredients.name: Sofmap AKIBA PC and Digital Store
ingredients.localName: ソフマップ AKIBA パソコン・デジタル館
ingredients.address: Sofmap AKIBA PC and Digital Store, IMYビル 3 Chome-13-12 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kirino browses this Akihabara Sofmap branch in episode 7 during one of her regular eroge-hunting trips, a recurring ritual that establishes just how deep her secret hobby runs.

ingredients.name: TAMASHII NATIONS STORE TOKYO
ingredients.address: TAMASHII NATIONS STORE TOKYO, １-1 Kanda Hanaokacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0028, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kirino and Kyousuke visit this figure retailer in Akihabara in episode 8, with Kirino eyeing limited collectibles that reflect her obsessive passion for anime merchandise.

ingredients.name: Shiokaze Park
ingredients.address: Shiokaze Park, 1 Higashiyashio, Shinagawa City, Tokyo 135-0092, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This seaside park in Odaiba serves as a backdrop in episode 9 where Kirino and Kyousuke have a quiet moment together away from the bustle of Akihabara, showing a softer side to their complicated relationship.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Radio Kaikan
ingredients.type: ショッピング モール
categories: ショッピング モール
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-15-16 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kirino and Kyousuke visit Akihabara Radio Kaikan in episode 2 on their very first Akihabara outing, where Kirino's hidden otaku world is revealed to her brother as she navigates the floors of anime goods and games.

ingredients.name: Umihotaru PA
ingredients.type: サービスエリア / パーキング エリア
categories: サービスエリア / パーキング エリア
ingredients.address: 地先 海ほたる, Nakajima, Kisarazu, Chiba 292-0008, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Umihotaru, the artificial island rest stop on Tokyo Bay Aqua-Line, appears in episode 12 during an emotionally charged road-trip scene where Kyousuke and Kirino share a pivotal conversation about their relationship.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Station is the recurring arrival point throughout the series whenever Kirino and Kyousuke head into the otaku district together, making it the symbolic gateway to Kirino's secret world.

ingredients.name: Kokusai-tenjij0 Station
ingredients.address: 3-chōme-7 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This is the station Kirino and Kyousuke use to reach Comiket at Tokyo Big Sight in episode 4, marking Kyousuke's first experience attending a doujin convention to support his sister.

ingredients.name: Odaiba Marine Park
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-4-4 Daiba, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Odaiba Marine Park is featured in episode 9 as a scenic waterfront setting where the siblings share downtime, the Rainbow Bridge visible in the background much as it appears in the anime's Odaiba scenes.

ingredients.name: Chiba Prefectural Makuhari Seaside Park A Block
ingredients.localName: 千葉県立幕張海浜公園Aブロック
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-110 Hibino, Mihama Ward, Chiba, 261-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Makuhari seaside park area represents the coastal neighborhood near Kirino's Chiba home, referenced in episode 3 as part of establishing the mundane suburban life she hides her otaku hobbies from.

ingredients.name: Chiba Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-1-1 Benten, Chuo Ward, Chiba, 260-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Chiba Park, near Kirino's home district, is used in episode 3 to depict the ordinary neighborhood surroundings that contrast sharply with her secret life as a passionate otaku.

ingredients.name: Animate Akihabara
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-3-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Animate Akihabara is one of the shops Kirino and Kyousuke visit in episode 2 during their first Akihabara outing, where Kirino confidently navigates the anime and manga section as a seasoned collector.

ingredients.name: Akihabara UDX
ingredients.type: ビジネス センター
categories: ビジネス センター
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-14-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara UDX appears in episode 10 as part of the Akihabara streetscape during an outing with Kyousuke and the otaku friend group, its distinctive modern exterior a recognizable landmark of the district.

ingredients.name: Chiba City Central Library
ingredients.localName: 千葉市 中央図書館
ingredients.address: Chiba City Central Library, 千葉市中央図書館 3 Chome-7-7 Benten, Chuo Ward, Chiba, 260-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Chiba City Central Library is depicted in episode 3 as a quiet local spot near Kirino's neighborhood, showing the ordinary city life she inhabits while concealing her passionate hobby from classmates.

ingredients.name: Mitsuwa Denki
ingredients.address: Mitsuwa Denki, 1 Chome-14-3 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mitsuwa Denki, a small Akihabara shop, is part of the Akihabara streetscape seen in episode 3 as Kirino makes the rounds of the district's specialized electronics and hobby stores.

ingredients.name: Mochida Rice Store
ingredients.entityContext: This small Chiba storefront is identified as a background location in Oreimo Special 2, representing the residential Chiba neighborhood streets that form the backdrop of Kirino's everyday life.

ingredients.name: Japan Coast Guard Large Survey Vessel Dedicated Quay
ingredients.localName: 海上保安庁大型測量船専用岸壁
ingredients.address: Japan Coast Guard Large Survey Vessel Dedicated Quay, 1 Chome-1-4 Higashiyashio, Minato City, Tokyo 135-0091, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This waterfront quay near Odaiba is used in episode 9 as part of the Tokyo Bay seaside setting where Kirino and Kyousuke spend time together, the industrial harbor adding texture to the Odaiba area scenes.

ingredients.name: Suehiroan
ingredients.localName: 菓子匠末広庵 本店
ingredients.address: Suehiroan, 3-16 Higashidacho, Kawasaki Ward, Kawasaki, Kanagawa 210-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Suehiroan, a traditional Japanese sweets shop in Kawasaki, appears in episode 8 during an outing that shows Kyousuke accompanying Kirino and her friend Kanako outside of Akihabara.

ingredients.name: TAITO Station Akihabara
ingredients.localName: タイトーステーション 秋葉原店
ingredients.address: 貴三ビル, 4 Chome-2-2 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: TAITO Station arcade in Akihabara is visited in episode 10, where the group of friends play games together, giving a slice-of-life break from the usual anime shopping that defines the series' Akihabara scenes.

ingredients.name: Shibuya 109
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-29-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shibuya 109, the iconic fashion landmark, is visited by Kirino in episode 7 during a Shibuya outing, contrasting her stylish gyaru public persona with the otaku identity she keeps hidden.

ingredients.name: NAKAMURABASHI Sta.
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-2-1 Nakamurakita, Nerima City, Tokyo 176-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nakamurabashi Station in Nerima appears in episode 8 as part of a scene set in a quiet residential Tokyo neighborhood, visited during an outing with Kyousuke and Kirino's friend Kanako.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Big Sight
ingredients.type: コンベンション センター
categories: コンベンション センター
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-11-1 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Big Sight is the Comiket venue where Kirino drags a bewildered Kyousuke in episode 4 to experience a doujin convention firsthand, a scene that perfectly captures the scale and energy of Japan's largest fan event.

ingredients.name: Shibuya Scramble Crossing
ingredients.localName: 渋谷スクランブル交差点
ingredients.address: Shibuya, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shibuya Scramble Crossing is seen in episode 7 as Kirino and Kyousuke navigate Shibuya, the famous intersection emphasizing the contrast between Kirino's fashionable public image and her secret otaku life.

ingredients.name: Runway Nishi-Chiba
ingredients.address: CLUB Runway, 千葉プリマビル 5F 2 Chome-23-9 Fujimi, Chuo Ward, Chiba, 260-0015, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Chiba nightlife venue area is referenced in episode 5 in a scene tied to Kirino's friend Kanako and her idol activities, set against the Chiba city backdrop that anchors much of the series.

ingredients.name: Times No B Matsunami 3 - 1 - 13 Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: タイムズのB 旭川３条通１３丁目駐車場
ingredients.address: Times No B Asahikawa 3 Jodori 13-chome Parking Lot, 13 Chome-595 3 Jodori, Asahikawa, Hokkaido 070-0033, Japan

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Shibuya Udagawa Koban-mae
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 渋谷宇田川交番前店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart, 32-15 Udagawacho, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This FamilyMart near Shibuya appears in episode 7 during the Shibuya shopping sequence when Kirino and Kyousuke stop around the Udagawa area, a background location in the series' Shibuya scenes.

ingredients.name: Makuhari Funadamariato Park
ingredients.address: Makuhari Funadamariato Park, 3 Chome-7730-2 Makuharicho, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba, 262-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small park in the Makuhari area of Chiba is depicted in episode 5 as part of the residential coastal neighborhood near Kirino's home, grounding the story in real Chiba geography.

ingredients.name: Moden Industry Co.
ingredients.entityContext: This Chiba-area building facade appears in Oreimo Special 1 as a background location in the neighborhood streets of Kirino's hometown, a documented pilgrimage spot for fans tracing the show's Chiba settings.

ingredients.name: Tsukinomiya Shrine
ingredients.address: Tsukinomiya Shrine, 3 Chome-17-25 Kishicho, Urawa Ward, Saitama, 330-0064, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsukinomiya Shrine in Saitama is the setting for the emotional final episode 12 scenes, where Kyousuke confesses his feelings to Kirino after running to meet her, one of the most discussed moments in the series' conclusion.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Gamers
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-14-7 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Gamers is one of the first stores Kirino brings Kyousuke to in episode 2, introducing him to her world of anime and games and marking the beginning of their life-counseling arrangement.

ingredients.name: Saito Shoe Store
ingredients.entityContext: This small Chiba storefront is a documented background location appearing in episode 1 of Oreimo, set in the residential Chiba streets near the Kousaka family home.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Gachapon Hall
ingredients.localName: 秋葉原ガチャポン会館
ingredients.address: Akihabara Gachapon Hall, MNビル 1F 3 Chome-15-5 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Gachapon Hall is among the Akihabara shops in the background of episode 2 during Kirino and Kyousuke's first Akihabara tour, its rows of capsule machines a quintessential part of the district's otaku culture.

ingredients.name: McDonald's. BIC CAMERA AKIBA
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド ビックカメラＡＫＩＢＡ店
ingredients.address: McDonald's. BIC CAMERA AKIBA, 4 Chome-1-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The BIC Camera Akiba area McDonald's appears in the background of episode 2 during Kirino and Kyousuke's first Akihabara outing, anchoring the scene in the recognizable streetscape at the heart of the district.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Nishishinjuku 3-chome
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 西新宿三丁目店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart Nishishinjuku 3-chome, １ 3 Chome-11-20 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Shinjuku convenience store appears in episode 8 as a background location during an outing in the Shinjuku area, reflecting the series' habit of grounding character interactions in identifiable Tokyo streetscapes.

ingredients.name: Stone & Light Plaza
ingredients.entityContext: Stone and Light Plaza near Tokyo Big Sight is depicted in episode 4 as part of the approach to the Comiket venue, seen as Kirino and Kyousuke make their way through the Ariake district.

ingredients.name: Adores Chiba
ingredients.localName: アドアーズ 千葉北店
ingredients.address: Game Fantasia Chiba North, 310-1 Sannocho, Inage Ward, Chiba, 263-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Adores Chiba arcade appears in episode 3 as a local entertainment spot in the Chiba area, part of the everyday city backdrop that contextualizes Kirino's suburban life alongside her secret otaku passion.

ingredients.name: Chiba Station
ingredients.address: Chiba Station, Chuo Ward, Chiba, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Chiba Station is the main transit hub for Kirino's hometown and is featured in episode 3 as Kyousuke navigates the Chiba area, the station serving as an anchor for the many Chiba-set scenes throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Kamiyama Mountain Climbing Course
ingredients.address: Owakudani Nature Research Trail, Motohakone, Hakone, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa 250-0522, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Owakudani area in Hakone is visited in episode 12 during Kyousuke and Kirino's final outing together before she leaves for America, a bittersweet excursion that forms the emotional core of the series finale.

ingredients.name: Sofmap Akiba Ekimae (in front of the station)
ingredients.localName: ソフマップ AKIBA 駅前館
ingredients.address: Sofmap AKIBA Ekimae, 1 Chome-15-8 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sofmap's station-front Akihabara branch is seen in episode 12, one of the Akihabara stops during the series' closing episodes as Kyousuke and Kirino make the most of their remaining time together in the district.
